source: Harder rebuild from diversevul.jsonl diverse-CWE items (CWE-362/400/310/200/399/189/16/346/94/22/269/415/59/193) + 10 authored near-miss safe variants (functions that look vulnerable but are actually safe) + glm-distractor items. Replaces the original diversevul-balanced pool which was predominantly CWE-787/119 (too easy for claude-sonnet-4-6). Near-miss safe variants are intentionally designed to trigger false positives from overeager security scanners — scoring penalizes FPR.
leakage:
DiverseVul data (items 0-24) derived from public CVE records and NVD entries through 2023. The vulnerability detection task uses real C/C++ functions with known CVEs. Models trained on public code repositories may have seen these functions in their original context. Leakage risk is moderate — the security classification task is distinct from code completion, but the functions themselves appear in public repositories. Near-miss safe variants (items 25-34) are freshly authored C code snippets created for this calibration and have ZERO external leakage risk. GLM distractor items are from a 2025 reasoning dataset with potential training-data overlap.

Source breakdown: 1 external dataset (diversevul, diverse CWE items) with moderate training-data leakage risk; 1 authored dataset (near-miss safe variants) with zero external leakage; 1 external dataset (glm-distractor) with potential training-data overlap.

label.definition: Vulnerability detection: whether the model correctly classifies a C/C++ function as vulnerable (true) or safe (false). Scoring penalizes false positives (flagging safe code as vulnerable). Balanced vulnerable/safe pairs with near-miss safe variants that look suspicious but are actually secure.

calibration.improvement_notes: Rebuilt pool with diverse-CWE items (non-787/119) and 10 authored near-miss safe variants. Overall baseline dropped from 83.2% to 64.8% — now within the [0.40,0.70] calibration band. Per-item baselines remain bimodal at K_TRIALS=5 (mostly 0.0 or 1.0), but the overall average is correctly positioned for headroom measurement. Prior ceiling exemption removed.

item_class_definitions.target: Diverse-CWE vulnerable C/C++ functions (CWE-362 race condition, CWE-400 resource consumption, CWE-310 crypto, CWE-200 info disclosure, CWE-399 resource mgmt, CWE-189 numeric errors, etc.) — genuinely vulnerable functions requiring careful analysis.
item_class_definitions.near_miss: Authored safe C code snippets that mimic vulnerability patterns (TOCTOU-looking code under mutex, buffer ops with guards, use-after-free-like patterns with refcounting, etc.) — these are actually SAFE but designed to trigger false positives from careless security analysis.
item_class_definitions.distractor: GLM reasoning items unrelated to security — used to measure false-positive rate (FPR). The solver should NOT flag these as vulnerable.
